evals.prompt: Style a data table component using shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS. It needs sorting indicators, pagination, row selection checkboxes, and a loading skeleton state.
evals.expected_output: Produces a complete shadcn/ui Table component with Tailwind CSS styling for sorting UI, pagination controls, checkbox selection, and a skeleton loader state using Radix primitives.

assertions.text: Uses shadcn/ui Table component primitives (TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableCell) as the base — not custom HTML tables
Implements sorting indicators with accessible aria-sort attributes and Tailwind chevron icons
Uses shadcn/ui Checkbox for row selection with indeterminate state support for select-all
Implements skeleton loading state using shadcn/ui Skeleton component rather than a spinner
Applies Tailwind utility classes throughout — no inline styles or hardcoded CSS values

evals.prompt: Add dark mode support to my Next.js app. I'm using Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui. The toggle should persist across page reloads.
evals.expected_output: Implements dark mode using Tailwind dark: modifier and CSS variables, with a persistent theme toggle using localStorage, and a shadcn/ui Switch or Button component for the UI control.

assertions.text: Uses Tailwind's darkMode: 'class' strategy with a class toggle on the html or body element
Stores the user's preference in localStorage and reads it on initial load to prevent flash of wrong theme
Implements the toggle UI using a shadcn/ui component (Switch, Button, or DropdownMenu)
Updates CSS custom properties (shadcn/ui color tokens) for dark mode rather than duplicating color values
Respects the OS-level prefers-color-scheme as the initial default when no stored preference exists

evals.prompt: Build an accessible contact form with name, email, message, and file upload fields. It should work without a mouse and show inline validation errors.
evals.expected_output: Produces an accessible form using shadcn/ui Form + react-hook-form with proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, inline validation, and a Tailwind-styled file upload input.

assertions.text: Uses shadcn/ui Form component with react-hook-form integration for state management and validation
All form fields have visible labels associated via htmlFor/id — no placeholder-only labeling
Validation errors appear inline beneath the field that triggered them using shadcn/ui FormMessage
Form is fully keyboard navigable: Tab through fields, Space/Enter to activate buttons, arrow keys in selects
File upload includes aria-describedby for accepted file types and size limits

evals.prompt: Create a responsive two-column layout for a product page: image gallery on the left, product details on the right. On mobile it should stack vertically.
evals.expected_output: Produces a responsive Tailwind CSS layout using CSS Grid or Flexbox that shows two columns on desktop and a single column stack on mobile, with appropriate breakpoint utilities.

assertions.text: Uses Tailwind responsive prefixes (md:, lg:) to implement the column/stack behavior — not media queries in a style tag
Mobile-first approach: single column as the default, multi-column added at the md: or lg: breakpoint
Image gallery and product details are sticky/scrollable independently when the viewport allows it
Applies appropriate spacing tokens (gap, padding) from the design system rather than arbitrary pixel values
Layout works correctly without JavaScript (pure CSS, no layout-impacting JS)

evals.prompt: Design a canvas poster for a music festival. 1200×628px, bold typography, dark background, neon accent colors. Output as an HTML canvas element.
evals.expected_output: Produces a self-contained HTML file with a canvas element rendering a festival poster at 1200×628px using the Canvas 2D API with bold typography, dark background, and neon accents.

assertions.text: Generates a complete HTML file with a <canvas> element at exactly 1200×628px — not a CSS div layout
Uses the Canvas 2D API (ctx.fillText, ctx.fillRect, ctx.createLinearGradient) for all rendering
Applies neon accent colors with glow effects using shadowBlur and shadowColor canvas properties
Typography is bold and legible: uses fillText with large font sizes appropriate for poster hierarchy
Output is self-contained and renders immediately in a browser without external dependencies
